title: cuscuta corniculata
intro: Cuscuta corniculata, also known as Dodder or Gulari, is a parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants, draining their nutrients, and ultimately killing them. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cuscuta corniculata include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating symptoms of rheumatism and arthritis. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Indian medicine as a paste or powder to treat various health conditions.
